story.title: Dr. Johansson and the Case of the Constant Weight
story.text:
Hello! I am Dr. Hannah Johansson. I study how matter changes. These changes are called phase transitions. Let's find out if matter disappears when it changes.

First, I put an ice cube in a sealed glass jar. An ice cube is solid water. I use a scale to weigh the jar with the ice inside. I write down the weight.

Next, I gently warm the jar. The ice melts into liquid water. This is a phase change. The solid becomes a liquid at its melting point. I weigh the jar again. The weight is exactly the same!

Now, I add more heat to the jar. The liquid water turns into a gas called steam. This is called evaporation. It is another phase change. The jar looks empty. But is it?

I weigh the jar one last time. The weight is still the same! No matter was lost. This shows the law of conservation of matter. The matter did not disappear. It just changed its form from solid, to liquid, to gas.
think_abouts: What happens to the water particles when ice melts?
Why was it important for Dr. Johansson to use a sealed jar?
story.go_deeper: What do you think would happen to the melting point if we added salt to the ice cube?

margin_defs.definition: Closed tightly so nothing can get in or out.
A tool used to measure how heavy something is.

check_prompts: When the ice melted, did the weight of the jar change?

vocabulary.definition: When matter switches between its solid, liquid, or gas form.
vocabulary.use_sentence: When an ice cube melts, it is going through a phase change.

vocabulary.definition: The science rule that says matter cannot be created or destroyed, only changed.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Because of the conservation of matter, the weight of the water stayed the same.

vocabulary.definition: The special temperature where a solid turns into a liquid.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The melting point of water is 0 degrees Celsius.

vocabulary.definition: The process when a liquid turns into a gas.
vocabulary.use_sentence: After the rain, evaporation made the puddles on the street dry up.

discussion_cards.prompt: If you leave an open cup of water on a windowsill, the water level goes down after a few days. Does this break the rule of conservation of matter? Why or why not?
discussion_cards.frame: This does not break the rule because the water...

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Johansson studies how matter changes. What is another example of a phase change you see in your everyday life?
discussion_cards.frame: One phase change I see is when...

discussion_cards.prompt: Why do you think a pressure cooker, which traps steam and increases pressure, helps food cook faster?
discussion_cards.frame: A pressure cooker might cook food faster because...

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
